poem:
**At the Pump**

Four years since the needle kissed this figure—  
$4.18, like a bruise blooming on the forecourt.  
Texas shrugs; California flinches.  
In the glare, we feed the mouth of a metal animal  
with notes, with hope, with curses.  
Somewhere, men argue over a narrow throat of sea,  
and our tanks empty on schedule.
prompt: You are the poet Simon Armitage. You woke up this morning feeling creative. You have just read this story in the newspaper: "US gas prices surge to highest level in four years, averaging $4.15 a gallon US gas prices rose to their highest level in four years on Thursday, reaching an average $4.18 a gallon at the pump as US-Israeli peace talks with Iran remain at a standstill. The last time average US gas prices breached $4.15 a gallon was in April 2022, when oil prices soared shortly after Russia invaded Ukraine. Average gas prices are now $1 higher than just a year ago, when they were closer to $3.15 a gallon. Average gas prices vary heavily by states, with oil-producing states seeing averages as much as $2 a gallon lower than states that import gas. In Texas, gas is $3.72 a gallon while California sees an average of $5.96 a gallon. Related: BP profits more than double as oil prices soar in Iran war By Tuesday morning, Brent crude, the global benchmark, hit $111 a barrel, lower than its high of $119 a barrel that was seen last month but nearly 60% higher than averages seen before the start of the war. WTI crude, the US benchmark, was near $100 a barrel on Tuesday morning. Oil prices went up on Tuesday after news that negotiators remain gridlocked over talks to reopen the strait of Hormuz, where a fifth of the world’s oil and natural gas would typically pass through. Donald Trump reportedly told advisers on Monday he is not happy with Iran’s proposal to reopen the strait, which would require the". Write a poem in YOUR OWN DISTINCTIVE STYLE, no more than 60 words long. You may add a one line title at the top if you like.
